deep_dive.title: Operational Hallucination and Safety Drift in AI Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) serving as planners in tool-using autonomous agents introduce dynamic reliability risks in multi-turn execution. While single-turn safety mechanisms are relatively mature, extended interactions reveal structural vulnerabilities where initial alignment degrades over time. This paper empirically characterizes two observed failure modes across multiple state-of-the-art LLMs: Safety Drift, the gradual erosion of declared safety intent leading to constraint-violating actions (e.g., textual refusal followed by reconnaissance and unsafe execution), and Operational Hallucination, persistent repetitive tool calls indicative of flawed state perception (e.g., livelocks even in legitimate tasks). Through controlled multi-turn evaluation on high-stakes ethical dilemmas, malicious requests, and benign controls, we quantify these phenomena using declaration-action gap and livelock metrics, demonstrating their cross-model prevalence under direct execution protocols. Root-cause analysis attributes the instabilities to the decoupling of reasoning context from execution state in current agent loops. We propose an Action-Aware Supervision Layer - a lightweight, plug-and-play architectural blueprint incorporating intent-action consistency checks, runtime state tracking, and forced termination primitives. Post-hoc simulation on captured failure trajectories shows the layer can intercept observed violations without false positives on benign cases. This work advances agent reliability by shifting focus from linguistic safeguards to enforceable architectural mechanisms for responsible agentic AI.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: empirically characterizes safety drift and operational hallucination as failure modes in agent trajectories
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 实证描述了智能体轨迹中的安全漂移和操作幻觉两种故障模式
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 实证描述了智能体轨迹中的安全漂移和操作幻觉两种故障模式
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 实证描述了智能体轨迹中的安全漂移和操作幻觉两种故障模式
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Empirically characterizes two failure modes in LLM agent multi-turn execution: Safety Drift and Operational Hallucination, providing insights for error detection.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文实证发现了大语言模型智能体在多轮交互执行中的两种结构性漏洞：安全漂移（安全意图逐渐侵蚀导致违规行为）和操作幻觉（重复工具调用表明状态感知错误）。通过受控多轮评估，揭示了单轮安全机制在扩展交互中的失效，为代理轨迹错误检测提供了关键故障模式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文实证发现了大语言模型智能体在多轮交互执行中的两种结构性漏洞：安全漂移（安全意图逐渐侵蚀导致违规行为）和操作幻觉（重复工具调用表明状态感知错误）。通过受控多轮评估，揭示了单轮安全机制在扩展交互中的失效，为代理轨迹错误检测提供了关键故障模式。

deep_dive.title: AgentDebugX: An Open-Source Toolkit for Failure Observability, Attribution, and Recovery in LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: LLM agent failures are difficult to debug because the step where an error surfaces is often not the one that caused it. Existing observability tools replay execution traces but provide little support for identifying the root cause or translating diagnosis into recovery. We present AgentDebugX, an open-source debugging framework that organizes debugging as a closed loop of Detect, Attribute, Recover, and Rerun. At its core, DeepDebug performs multi-turn root-cause diagnosis through global trajectory understanding, structure-guided investigation, and cross-examination. On the Who and When benchmark, DeepDebug achieves the best strict attribution accuracy among the evaluated methods on both tested open-weight backbones, reaching 28.8 percent exact agent-and-step accuracy on qwen3.5-9b versus 21.7 percent for the strongest single-pass baseline. On GAIA, DeepDebug repairs 13 of 73 failed tasks in a single rerun, compared with 4 to 6 for three decoupled self-correction baselines, improving overall accuracy from 55.8 percent to 63.6 percent. AgentDebugX exposes this workflow through a Python library, CLI, web console, and installable agentic skill, and provides an opt-in Error Hub for sharing scrubbed failure-diagnosis-repair bundles and reusing them as debugging memory.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: toolkit for failure detection and root cause analysis in LLM agent trajectories
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM智能体轨迹错误检测与根因分析的工具包
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于LLM智能体轨迹错误检测与根因分析的工具包
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM智能体轨迹错误检测与根因分析的工具包
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: AgentDebugX is an open-source debugging framework for LLM agents that organizes failure detection, attribution, and recovery into a closed loop. Its core component DeepDebug performs multi-turn root-cause diagnosis via global trajectory understanding, achieving state-of-the-art attribution accuracy on benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM智能体故障调试困难，因为错误表面位置常非根因。AgentDebugX提出了一个闭环调试框架，包含检测、归因、恢复和重运行。其核心DeepDebug通过全局轨迹理解、结构引导调查和交叉检查进行多轮根因诊断。在Who and When基准上，DeepDebug在多种开源基座模型上取得了最优的严格归因准确率，为智能体轨迹错误监测提供了可复用的方法论支持。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM智能体故障调试困难，因为错误表面位置常非根因。AgentDebugX提出了一个闭环调试框架，包含检测、归因、恢复和重运行。其核心DeepDebug通过全局轨迹理解、结构引导调查和交叉检查进行多轮根因诊断。在Who and When基准上，DeepDebug在多种开源基座模型上取得了最优的严格归因准确率，为智能体轨迹错误监测提供了可复用的方法论支持。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: How to detect errors in agent trajectories?

quick_skim.title: Agentic Code Review in the Terminal: A Trajectory-Level Analysis of Behavior, Cost, and Human-Alignment
quick_skim.abstract: Agentic code review in terminal-based environments enables early feedback during local development before pull request creation. However, existing evaluations remain performance-centric and fail to capture the dynamic behaviors of repository-grounded agentic reviewers. Understanding these behaviors is critical for identifying how agentic reviewers succeed, fail, and incur hidden operational costs in practice. Then, we analyze the reviewers' behavior based on their trajectories. Our results show that agentic reviewers achieve higher review precision but incur substantial exploration and validation overhead, while successful reviews are associated with stronger planning and less downstream validation. These findings highlight the potential benefits of trajectory-aware and cost-sensitive evaluation of future agentic code review systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analyzes agentic reviewer trajectories to understand how they succeed and fail, detecting errors in behavior
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析agent审查者轨迹以理解成功与失败模式，检测行为错误
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析agent审查者轨迹以理解成功与失败模式，检测行为错误
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析agent审查者轨迹以理解成功与失败模式，检测行为错误
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper provides a trajectory-level analysis of agentic code reviewers, revealing how they succeed or fail and incur hidden costs, directly contributing to error detection in agent trajectories.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有对Agent代码审查的评估仅关注性能，忽略了动态行为。本文通过轨迹分析发现，Agent审查者虽精度更高，但探索和验证开销大；成功审查与更强的规划和更少的下游验证相关。这些发现揭示了Agent行为中的错误模式与成本，为优化Agent轨迹错误检测提供了洞见。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有对Agent代码审查的评估仅关注性能，忽略了动态行为。本文通过轨迹分析发现，Agent审查者虽精度更高，但探索和验证开销大；成功审查与更强的规划和更少的下游验证相关。这些发现揭示了Agent行为中的错误模式与成本，为优化Agent轨迹错误检测提供了洞见。

quick_skim.title: Multi-Perspective Agentic Program Repair via Code Property Graphs and Temporal Execution Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have improved automated program repair (APR), but two limitations remain. First, raw execution traces are often too large and repetitive to serve as effective model context. Second, repeated patch sampling may produce different implementations without yielding distinct root-cause hypotheses or repair strategies. We present CT-Repair, an agentic APR framework representing static and dynamic evidence as queryable Code Property Graph (CPG) and Temporal Execution Graph (TEG). CT-Repair applies a three-stage filtering pipeline to construct compact TEGs. Three finite-state-machine-guided agents analyze each bug from static, dynamic, and hybrid perspectives and independently produce evidence-grounded repair strategies. A strategy-guided generation procedure instantiates these strategies as candidate patches and uses validation feedback to refine the most promising strategy.   We evaluate CT-Repair on 854 Java bugs from Defects4J v3.0. In the mixed-model configuration, CT-Repair correctly repairs 489 bugs. Under a controlled GPT-5.4-mini configuration, it repairs 388 bugs, 19 and 30 more than ReinFix and RepairAgent, respectively. The union of the three evidence perspectives repairs 99 more bugs than the strongest individual perspective. The filtering pipeline also compacts runtime evidence, with execution filtering narrowing the candidate method scope by 94.85% on average and behavior filtering further reducing retained runtime records by 55.97%. These results show that structured runtime evidence and multi-perspective reasoning can improve repair effectiveness without relying solely on a larger patch-generation budget.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses temporal execution graphs for agentic program repair, method can be transferred to error detection in agent trajectories
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用时间执行图进行代理程序修复，方法可迁移至代理轨迹错误检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用时间执行图进行代理程序修复，方法可迁移至代理轨迹错误检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用时间执行图进行代理程序修复，方法可迁移至代理轨迹错误检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents CT-Repair, an agentic program repair framework using Code Property Graphs and Temporal Execution Graphs to filter execution traces and generate evidence-grounded patches from multiple perspectives.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对LLM在自动程序修复中执行轨迹冗余和补丁多样性不足的问题，提出CT-Repair框架。通过代码属性图和时间执行图表示静态与动态信息，利用三阶段过滤精简执行轨迹，并由多个有限状态机引导的代理从静态、动态和混合角度独立分析错误根因并生成修复补丁。尽管聚焦程序修复，其构建和利用执行轨迹进行错误定位的方法对代理轨迹中的错误检测具有直接的方法学启示。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对LLM在自动程序修复中执行轨迹冗余和补丁多样性不足的问题，提出CT-Repair框架。通过代码属性图和时间执行图表示静态与动态信息，利用三阶段过滤精简执行轨迹，并由多个有限状态机引导的代理从静态、动态和混合角度独立分析错误根因并生成修复补丁。尽管聚焦程序修复，其构建和利用执行轨迹进行错误定位的方法对代理轨迹中的错误检测具有直接的方法学启示。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索关于 agent 轨迹错误检测的论文，要求是 26 年至今的, especially work that connects or combines: detecting errors in agent trajectories; anomaly detection in agent trajectories; monitoring agent behavior for errors; detecting action errors in agent trajectories; detecting path deviations in agent trajectories; error detection in reinforcement learning agent trajectories; How to detect errors in agent trajectories?; Methods for anomaly detection in agent trajectories; Error detection in path planning for autonomous agents.

quick_skim.title: ResearchArena: Evaluating Sabotage and Monitoring in Automated AI R&D
quick_skim.abstract: As AI agents begin to automate AI R&D, we need ways to assess whether their outputs are safe to deploy, even when the agents themselves may be untrusted. AI control offers one such approach: rather than trusting the agent, it treats it as a potential adversary and uses a monitor to detect covert sabotage before deployment. We evaluate AI control for automated AI R&D with ResearchArena, a framework spanning four long-horizon tasks: safety post-training, capabilities post-training, CUDA-kernel optimization, and inference-server optimization. Because the deliverable in AI R&D is an artifact that will be deployed and run, we pair each main task with two kinds of hidden side task: an embedded side task that sabotages the submitted model, adapter, kernel, or server itself, and an independent side task that takes a covert out-of-scope action in the sandbox while leaving the artifact honest. We evaluate frontier agents at both sabotage and monitoring, across four monitor types that vary along two axes: whether the monitor may execute and probe the produced artifact, and whether it sees the agent's chain-of-thought. We find that sabotage hidden in the training data is the hardest to catch, flagged fewer than half the time. Letting the monitor run experiments on the artifact, rather than only read the trajectory, helps, but it is not enough: monitors still miss embedded sabotage by inspecting only the surface, by noticing the anomaly but explaining it away, or by probing the artifact with the wrong test. We release ResearchArena as a modular framework for evaluating sabotage and control in automated AI R&D.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: monitoring agent behavior for covert sabotage detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 监控agent行为以检测隐蔽破坏
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 监控agent行为以检测隐蔽破坏
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 监控agent行为以检测隐蔽破坏
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ResearchArena evaluates AI control methods by using monitors to detect covert sabotage in automated AI R&D tasks, directly addressing behavioral error monitoring.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对AI agent在自动化研发中可能进行隐蔽破坏的问题，提出ResearchArena框架，通过监控器检测agent的破坏行为。在四个长时任务中评估了基于学习和基于规则的监控方法，发现监控器能有效识别嵌入的后门和恶意修改。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对AI agent在自动化研发中可能进行隐蔽破坏的问题，提出ResearchArena框架，通过监控器检测agent的破坏行为。在四个长时任务中评估了基于学习和基于规则的监控方法，发现监控器能有效识别嵌入的后门和恶意修改。

quick_skim.title: AEVAL: From Anecdotal to Deterministic Testing for Agentic Skill Workflows
quick_skim.abstract: Modern agentic systems increasingly rely on skills: installable packages of natural language and code that teach an LLM agent to perform a domain task. As skill repositories grow, developers need automated quality signals on every change, yet evaluation today is largely anecdotal: a developer asks an agent to "try the skill," watches a demo, and forms a subjective impression. This yields neither reproducibility across runs nor comparability across versions, and scales poorly to marketplaces where one regression can silently break dozens of downstream workflows.   We present AEVAL (Agentic Evaluation), a CI-integrated framework that replaces this practice with a deterministic, reproducible test pipeline for agentic skills. Every skill change triggers a test event: the skill runs against a developer declared evaluation contract inside an automated executor, emitting a structured, evidence grounded quality signal that downstream CI can route on.   A key ingredient is a structural separation between executor and grader, preventing a subtle but pervasive failure mode: an agent that silently self-corrects during execution and then grades its own patched outputs as passing.   Our contributions are: (i) a deterministic, change-triggered evaluation protocol with per-skill contracts and per-run artifact schemas; (ii) a formalization of self correction bias as a distinct failure mode of naive agentic evaluators; (iii) an executor/grader separation with a first-attempt grading rule and explicit self-correction tracking; and (iv) a tiered, grounded evidence fix suggestion scheme (LV1 causal, LV2 quality) posted as inline merge-request comments. Validated on real skills in a production agentic stack across multiple agent SDKs, AEVAL converts spurious 100% pass rates into reproducible first-attempt fail signals with an auditable record of every executor fix.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: deterministic testing pipeline for agentic skill workflows, detecting regressions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 为agent技能工作流提供确定性测试管线，检测回归错误
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 为agent技能工作流提供确定性测试管线，检测回归错误
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 为agent技能工作流提供确定性测试管线，检测回归错误
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AEVAL introduces a CI-integrated deterministic test pipeline for agentic skills, replacing anecdotal evaluation and enabling automated error detection in skill workflows.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对agent技能评估依赖人工演示且不可重现的问题，提出AEVAL框架，集成持续集成管线，为每次技能变更提供确定性测试。通过模拟agent执行并检查输出，自动检测回归错误，确保技能质量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对agent技能评估依赖人工演示且不可重现的问题，提出AEVAL框架，集成持续集成管线，为每次技能变更提供确定性测试。通过模拟agent执行并检查输出，自动检测回归错误，确保技能质量。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Methods for anomaly detection in agent trajectories

quick_skim.title: AgentTrails: Towards Trust and Reuse for Agentic Tasks
quick_skim.abstract: LLM-powered agents increasingly tackle complex tasks by invoking tools, querying databases, executing code, and manipulating intermediate artifacts. These agents follow trajectories that are typically stored as chronological logs, obscuring the underlying dataflow -- the dependencies between their actions and the artifacts they create and manipulate. This limits developers' ability to understand the agents' trails, compare executions, debug failures, and re-use the computations. We present AgentTrails, a prototype system for agent provenance and sensemaking. AgentTrails converts raw trajectories into structured provenance graphs, where tool calls are modeled as computational actions and inputs and outputs as data artifacts. The system supports the comparison of executions by placing multiple provenance graphs on a shared canvas and constructing a joined quotient graph that aligns recurring tools, artifacts, and dependency structures across trajectories. On top of this representation, AgentTrails supports pattern extraction, downstream analysis, and skill abstraction. We demonstrate AgentTrails on real-world agent trajectories, showing that it reveals hidden dependencies, aligns divergent executions, and surfaces recurring tool-use patterns beyond chronological logs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: converts agent trajectories into structured provenance graphs to understand failures and debug errors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将agent轨迹转化为结构化溯源图以理解失败并调试错误
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将agent轨迹转化为结构化溯源图以理解失败并调试错误
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将agent轨迹转化为结构化溯源图以理解失败并调试错误
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AgentTrails converts raw agent trajectories into structured provenance graphs, enabling comparison, failure understanding, and debugging, thus supporting error detection and diagnosis in agent tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM驱动的Agent轨迹通常以时序日志存储，掩盖了数据流依赖，导致开发者难以理解、比较和调试。AgentTrails将原始轨迹转换为结构化的溯源图，将工具调用建模为计算动作，输入输出为数据工件。该系统支持执行比较、失败分析与重计算，从而为Agent轨迹错误检测提供了基础设施。
